generatedSentence: The grizzled helmsman, a perpetual guest at the desert outpost, tended the outdoor grill, his fervid eyes scanning distant caravans leaving prints in the sand. He knew nowadays, any incoming info could spell trouble; a long-ago mistake involving a valentine invoice still felt ticklish, a florid scar. He'd been grappling with past tarnation, and new milestones forced him to consider tackling a full locking down of the hatches, preparing an oat and garlic stew. A new jeopardy loomed; an oily, monotone message, the lexicon of anarchy and drugs, made his gut itchier. He recognized the micro-network's unfortunate pattern, a rough system rooted in chaos, aiming for unification of syndicates, releasing a venom near his sanctuary. His organs depressed with great fright, drowning them. He sought justification for a decisive takeoff, needing to validate his next move neatly in the windy desert. An ugly, neurotic barbarian leader, whose past was a troubled film of ruthless barter, burst in demanding an actual return of stolen jewelry, asserting his ontological claim. His followers, a divergent group, moved like ants on an autobahn, creating a dramatic, volatile situation. The grizzled man, despite feeling vulnerable, maintained a tacit defiance, refusing to be among the losers in this battle for mastery. He saw the elephants of their power, a hickory club, but calmly set up mahjong, challenging the musician leader. With a final jolt, the barbarian felt a sliver of liberty as the old man, with a solo whistle, offered a sandwich by the waterhole, partially watered. His jobbing bookkeeper, noting the tense situation via social media, saw the interworking details, a snippet of new orthography. The old man, with a firm jaw, juked the conversation to communicate a cherrypicking deal. He threw a lump of gold, blunting their knives, averting tanking, leaving them free but with a faint whit of suspicion; the quiet hammers of consequences, like the raw vagina of their vulnerability, still resonant.
panelSentences: The grizzled helmsman, a perpetual guest at the desert outpost, tended the outdoor grill, his fervid eyes scanning distant caravans leaving prints in the sand. He knew nowadays, any incoming info could spell trouble; a long-ago mistake involving a valentine invoice still felt ticklish, a florid scar. He'd been grappling with past tarnation, and new milestones forced him to consider tackling a full locking down of the hatches, preparing an oat and garlic stew.

A new jeopardy loomed; an oily, monotone message, the lexicon of anarchy and drugs, made his gut itchier. He recognized the micro-network's unfortunate pattern, a rough system rooted in chaos, aiming for unification of syndicates, releasing a venom near his sanctuary. His organs depressed with great fright, drowning them. He sought justification for a decisive takeoff, needing to validate his next move neatly in the windy desert.

An ugly, neurotic barbarian leader, whose past was a troubled film of ruthless barter, burst in demanding an actual return of stolen jewelry, asserting his ontological claim. His followers, a divergent group, moved like ants on an autobahn, creating a dramatic, volatile situation. The grizzled man, despite feeling vulnerable, maintained a tacit defiance, refusing to be among the losers in this battle for mastery. He saw the elephants of their power, a hickory club, but calmly set up mahjong, challenging the musician leader.

With a final jolt, the barbarian felt a sliver of liberty as the old man, with a solo whistle, offered a sandwich by the waterhole, partially watered. His jobbing bookkeeper, noting the tense situation via social media, saw the interworking details, a snippet of new orthography. The old man, with a firm jaw, juked the conversation to communicate a cherrypicking deal. He threw a lump of gold, blunting their knives, averting tanking, leaving them free but with a faint whit of suspicion; the quiet hammers of consequences, like the raw vagina of their vulnerability, still resonant.
rawPanelSentences: The grizzled **helmsman**, a perpetual **guest** at the desert outpost, tended the outdoor **grill**, his **fervid** **eyes** scanning distant **caravans** leaving **prints** in the **sand**. He knew **nowadays**, any incoming **info** could spell trouble; a long-ago **mistake** involving a **valentine** **invoice** still felt **ticklish**, a **florid** scar. He'd been **grappling** with past **tarnation**, and new **milestones** forced him to consider **tackling** a full **locking** down of the **hatches**, preparing an **oat** and **garlic** stew.

A new **jeopardy** loomed; an **oily**, **monotone** message, the **lexicon** of **anarchy** and **drugs**, made his gut **itchier**. He recognized the **micro**-network's **unfortunate** pattern, a **rough** system **rooted** in chaos, aiming for **unification** of syndicates, releasing a **venom** **near** his sanctuary. His **organs** **depress**ed with **great** **fright**, drowning them. He sought **justification** for a decisive **takeoff**, needing to **validate** his next move **neatly** in the **windy** desert.

An **ugly**, **neurotic** **barbarian** leader, whose past was a **troubled** **film** of ruthless **barter**, burst in demanding an **actual** **return** of stolen **jewelry**, asserting his **ontological** claim. His followers, a **divergent** group, moved like **ants** on an **autobahn**, creating a **dramatic**, **volatile** situation. The grizzled man, despite feeling **vulnerable**, maintained a **tacit** defiance, refusing to be among the **losers** in this battle for **mastery**. He saw the **elephants** of their power, a **hickory** club, but calmly set up **mahjong**, challenging the **musician** leader.

With a final **jolt**, the barbarian felt a sliver of **liberty** as the old man, with a **solo** **whistle**, offered a **sandwich** by the **waterhole**, partially **watered**. His **jobbing** **bookkeeper**, noting the tense **situation** via **social media**, saw the **interworking** details, a **snippet** of new **orthography**. The old man, with a firm **jaw**, **juked** the conversation to **communicate** a **cherrypicking** deal. He threw a **lump** of gold, blunting their **knives**, averting **tanking**, leaving them **free** but with a faint **whit** of suspicion; the quiet **hammers** of consequences, like the raw **vagina** of their vulnerability, still resonant.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, gritty, realistic comic book art style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A craggy-faced elderly man with a long, braided white beard and a weathered leather duster. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The elderly man, tending a smoking grill outside a desert outpost, peers intently at distant, wind-swept dunes, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The elderly man, his face etched with concern, reads a data-slate displaying a faint, intricate map, close-up. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The elderly man stands stoically, confronting a hulking, scarred barbarian leader who gestures aggressively, medium shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The elderly man calmly places a gleaming lump of gold onto a rough wooden table, the barbarian leader looking surprised, eye-level shot. Cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. All characters visibly adult with mature features.

definitions.definition: A convoy or procession of travelers, their vehicles and cargo, and any pack animals, especially camels crossing a desert.
A furnished vehicle towed behind a car, etc., and used as a dwelling when stationary.
To travel in a caravan (procession).
definitions.example: The wedding party got in their cars and caravaned from the chapel to the reception hall.

definitions.definition: To travel and/or live in a caravan (vehicle).
definitions.example: When my parents retired they really got back into caravanning.

definitions.definition: A recipient of hospitality, specifically someone staying by invitation at the house of another.
definitions.example: The guests were let in by the butler.

definitions.definition: A patron or customer in a hotel etc.
definitions.example: Guests must vacate their rooms by 10 o'clock on their day of departure.

definitions.definition: An invited visitor or performer to an institution or to a broadcast.
definitions.example: The guest for the broadcast was a leading footballer.

definitions.definition: A user given temporary access to a system despite not having an account of their own.
Any insect that lives in the nest of another without compulsion and usually not as a parasite.
An inquiline.
To appear as a guest, especially on a broadcast
As a musician, to play as a guest, providing an instrument that a band/orchestra does not normally have in its line up (for instance, percussion in a string band)
To receive or entertain hospitably.

A grating; a grid of wire or a sheet of material with a pattern of holes or slots, usually used to protect something while allowing the passage of air and liquids. Typical uses: to allow air through a fan while preventing fingers or objects from passing; to allow people to talk to somebody, while preventing attack.
On a vehicle, a slotted cover as above, to protect and hide the radiator, while admitting air to cool it.
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative heat and a means of holding food under it; a broiler in US English
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative and convective heat and a means of holding food above it; a barbecue.
definitions.example: I put some peppers and mushrooms on the grill to go with dinner.

definitions.definition: Food (designed to be) cooked on a grill.
definitions.example: a packet of frozen cauliflower cheese grills

definitions.definition: A grillroom; a restaurant serving grilled food.
definitions.example: These coupons will get you a discount at Johnny's Bar and Grill.

definitions.definition: A type of jewelry worn on the front teeth.
(by extension) The front teeth regarded collectively.
To cook (food) on a grill; to barbecue.
definitions.example: Why don't we get together Saturday and grill some burgers?

definitions.definition: To cook food under the element of a stove or only under the top element of an oven – broil, salamander.
To interrogate; to question aggressively or harshly.
definitions.example: The police grilled him about his movements at the time of the crime.

definitions.definition: To feel very hot; to swelter.
To stamp or mark with a grill.
Sensitive or susceptible to being tickled.
definitions.example: She is ticklish only on her tummy and the bottoms of her feet.

definitions.definition: Touchy, sensitive, or delicate.
A passageway covered by an arch, particularly one made of masonry.
A doorway with a semilunar-shaped top.
Information.
A horizontal door in a floor or ceiling.
A trapdoor.
An opening in a wall at window height for the purpose of serving food or other items. A pass through.
definitions.example: The cook passed the dishes through the serving hatch.

definitions.definition: A small door in large mechanical structures and vehicles such as aircraft and spacecraft often provided for access for maintenance.
An opening through the deck of a ship or submarine
A gullet.
A frame or weir in a river, for catching fish.
A floodgate; a sluice gate.
A bedstead.
An opening into, or in search of, a mine.
To close with a hatch or hatches.
The act of hatching.
Development; disclosure; discovery.
(poultry) A group of birds that emerged from eggs at a specified time.
definitions.example: These pullets are from an April hatch.

definitions.definition: (often as mayfly hatch) The phenomenon, lasting 1–2 days, of large clouds of mayflies appearing in one location to mate, having reached maturity.
A birth, the birth records (in the newspaper) — compare the phrase "hatched, matched, and dispatched."
(of young animals) To emerge from an egg.
(of eggs) To break open when a young animal emerges from it.
To incubate eggs; to cause to hatch.
To devise.
To shade an area of (a drawing, diagram, etc.) with fine parallel lines, or with lines which cross each other (cross-hatch).
To cross; to spot; to stain; to steep.
Of or relating to actors or acting.
(by extension) Excessively dramatic or emotional, especially with the intention to draw attention.
An organ through which animals see (perceive surroundings via light).
definitions.example: Bright lights really hurt my eyes.

definitions.definition: The visual sense.
definitions.example: The car was quite pleasing to the eye, but impractical.

definitions.definition: The iris of the eye, being of a specified colour.
definitions.example: Brown, blue, green, hazel eyes.

definitions.definition: Attention, notice.
definitions.example: That dress caught her eye.

definitions.definition: The ability to notice what others might miss.
definitions.example: He has an eye for talent.

definitions.definition: A meaningful stare or look.
definitions.example: She was giving him the eye at the bar.

definitions.definition: A private eye: a privately hired detective or investigator.
A hole at the blunt end of a needle through which thread is passed.
The oval hole of an axehead through which the axehandle is fitted.
A fitting consisting of a loop of metal or other material, suitable for receiving a hook or the passage of a cord or line.
The relatively clear and calm center of a hurricane or other cyclonic storm.
A mark on an animal, such as a peacock or butterfly, resembling a human eye.
The dark spot on a black-eyed pea.
A reproductive bud in a potato.
The dark brown center of a black-eyed Susan flower.

A loop forming part of anything, or a hole through anything, to receive a rope, hook, pin, shaft, etc. — e.g. at the end of a tie bar in a bridge truss; through a crank; at the end of a rope; or through a millstone.
That which resembles the eye in relative importance or beauty.
Tinge; shade of colour.
One of the holes in certain kinds of cheese.
The circle in the centre of a volute.
The enclosed counter (negative space) of the small letter e.
(game of go) An empty point or group of points surrounded by one player's stones.
(usually plural) View or opinion.
definitions.example: This victory will make us great in the eyes of the world.

definitions.definition: To observe carefully or appraisingly.
definitions.example: After eyeing the document for half an hour, she decided not to sign it.

definitions.definition: To appear; to look.
A brood.
definitions.example: an eye of pheasants

definitions.definition: An error; a blunder.
A pitch which was intended to be pitched in a hard-to-hit location, but instead ends up in an easy-to-hit place.
To understand wrongly, taking one thing or person for another.
definitions.example: Don't mistake my kindness for weakness.

definitions.definition: To misunderstand (someone).
To commit an unintentional error; to do or think something wrong.
To take or choose wrongly.
A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
Books and other material created by printing presses, considered collectively or as a medium.
definitions.example: TV and the internet haven't killed print.

definitions.definition: Clear handwriting, especially, writing without connected letters as in cursive.
definitions.example: Write in print using block letters.

definitions.definition: The letters forming the text of a document.
definitions.example: The print is too small for me to read.

definitions.definition: A newspaper.
A visible impression on a surface.
definitions.example: Using a crayon, the girl made a print of the leaf under the page.

definitions.definition: A fingerprint.
definitions.example: Did the police find any prints at the scene?

definitions.definition: A footprint.
(visual art) A picture that was created in multiple copies by printing.
A photograph that has been printed onto paper from the negative.
A copy of a film that can be projected.
Cloth that has had a pattern of dye printed onto it.
A plaster cast in bas relief.
To produce one or more copies of a text or image on a surface, especially by machine; often used with out or off: print out, print off.
definitions.example: Print the draft double-spaced so we can mark changes between the lines.

definitions.definition: To produce a microchip (an integrated circuit) in a process resembling the printing of an image.
definitions.example: The circuitry is printed onto the semiconductor surface.

definitions.definition: To write very clearly, especially, to write without connecting the letters as in cursive.
definitions.example: I'm only in grade 2, so I only know how to print.

definitions.definition: To publish in a book, newspaper, etc.
definitions.example: How could they print an unfounded rumour like that?

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress (something) with coloured figures or patterns.
definitions.example: to print calico

definitions.definition: To fix or impress, as a stamp, mark, character, idea, etc., into or upon something.
To stamp something in or upon; to make an impression or mark upon by pressure, or as by pressure.
To display a string on the terminal.
To produce an observable value.
definitions.example: On March 16, 2020, the S&P printed at 2,386.13, one of the worst drops in history.

definitions.definition: The smallest part or particle imaginable; an iota.
definitions.example: He worked tirelessly to collect and wind a ball of string eight feet around, and it matters not one whit.

definitions.definition: A device designed to be placed in the mouth and blown, or driven by steam or some other mechanism, to make a whistling sound.
An act of whistling.
A shrill, high-pitched sound made by whistling.
Any high-pitched sound similar to the sound made by whistling.
definitions.example: the whistle of the wind in the trees

definitions.definition: A suit (from whistle and flute).
The mouth and throat; so called as being the organs of whistling.
To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.
definitions.example: Never whistle at a funeral.

definitions.definition: To make a similar sound by forcing air through a musical instrument or a pipe etc.
definitions.example: The stream train whistled as it passed by.

definitions.definition: To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.
definitions.example: A bullet whistled past.

definitions.definition: To send, signal, or call by a whistle.
Rock that is ground more finely than gravel, but is not as fine as silt (more formally, see grain sizes chart), forming beaches and deserts and also used in construction.
(often in the plural) A beach or other expanse of sand.
definitions.example: The Canadian tar sands are a promising source of oil.

definitions.definition: (circa 1920) Personal courage.
A particle from 62.5 microns to 2 mm in diameter, following the Wentworth scale.
A light beige colour, like that of typical sand.
A single grain of sand.
A moment or interval of time; the term or extent of one's life (referring to the sand in an hourglass).
Of a light beige colour, like that of typical sand.
To become fastened in place.
definitions.example: If you put the brakes on too hard, the wheels will lock.

definitions.definition: To fasten with a lock.
definitions.example: Remember to lock the door when you leave.

definitions.definition: To be capable of becoming fastened in place.
definitions.example: This door locks with a key.

definitions.definition: To intertwine or dovetail.
definitions.example: We locked arms and stepped out into the night.

definitions.definition: (break dancing) To freeze one's body or a part thereof in place.
definitions.example: a pop and lock routine

definitions.definition: To furnish (a canal) with locks.
To raise or lower (a boat) in a lock.
To seize (e.g. the sword arm of an antagonist) by turning the left arm around it, to disarm him.
To modify (a thread) so that users cannot make new posts in it.
(WMF jargon) To prevent a page from being edited by other users.
definitions.example: Frequently vandalized pages are generally locked to prevent further damage.

definitions.definition: The act by which something is locked.
The use of a lock or a mutex to restrict access to a part of the code to at most one process.
Having a rosy or pale red colour; ruddy.
Elaborately ornate; flowery.
(of a disorder, especially mental) In a blatant, vivid, or highly disorganized state.
Flourishing; in the bloom of health.
Intensely hot, emotional, or zealous.
At the present time; in the current era.
Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
definitions.example: The main forms of oat are meal and bran.

definitions.definition: Any of the numerous species, varieties, or cultivars of any of several similar grain plants in genus Avena.
definitions.example: The wild red oat is thought to be the ancestor of modern food oats.

definitions.definition: (usually as plural) The seeds of the oat, a grain, harvested as a food crop.
A simple musical pipe made of oat-straw.
To seize something and hold it firmly.
To wrestle or tussle.
(with with) To ponder and intensely evaluate a problem.
definitions.example: to grapple with one's conscience

definitions.definition: To fasten, as with a grapple; (by extension) to fix; to join indissolubly.
To climb (whether by means of a grapple and rope, or by hand, etc).
To use a grapple (for example to attempt to find, hook, and raise a net or cable).
To hook and raise with a grapple.
(gerund of grapple) An act in which something is grappled or grappled with.
A grappling hook or grappling iron.
A small anchor; a grapnel.
A plant, Allium sativum, related to the onion, having a pungent bulb much used in cooking.
To flavour with garlic
The act or process of damnation or reprobation; hell.
definitions.example: What in tarnation is going on?

definitions.definition: Someone or something that causes trouble; troublemaker.
Bothersome; devilish.
Generic intensifier.
Very; extremely.
Used to express anger, irritation, disappointment, annoyance, contempt, etc.
To force a person to the ground with the weight of one's own body, usually by jumping on top or slamming one's weight into him or her.
To face or deal with, attempting to overcome or fight down.
definitions.example: The government's measures to tackle crime were insufficient.

definitions.definition: To attempt to take away a ball.
To bring a ball carrier to the ground.
To "hit on" or pursue a person that one is interested in.
The process by which something is tackled or dealt with.
The tackle of a vessel.
The harness for drawing a carriage.
An expression of affection, especially romantic affection, usually in the form of greeting card, gift, or message given the object of one's affection, especially on February 14th.
definitions.example: Such an extravagant valentine was unexpected.

definitions.definition: A person to whom a valentine is given or from whom it is received, especially on February 14th.
definitions.example: Won't you be my valentine?

definitions.definition: The state of a society being without authorities or an authoritative governing body.
Anarchism; the political theory that a community is best organized by the voluntary cooperation of individuals, rather than by a government, which is regarded as being coercive by nature.
A chaotic and confusing absence of any form of political authority or government.
Confusion in general; disorder.
Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.
A substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or modify a chemical process in the body for a specific purpose.
definitions.example: Aspirin is a drug that reduces pain, acts against inflammation and lowers body temperature.

definitions.definition: A psychoactive substance, especially one which is illegal and addictive, ingested for recreational use, such as cocaine.
Anything, such as a substance, emotion or action, to which one is addicted.
definitions.example: 2010, Kesha Rose Sebert (Ke$ha), with Pebe Sebert and Joshua Coleman (Ammo), Your Love is My Drug

definitions.definition: Any commodity that lies on hand, or is not salable; an article of slow sale, or in no demand.
To administer intoxicating drugs to, generally without the recipient's knowledge or consent.
definitions.example: She suddenly felt strange, and only then realized she'd been drugged.

definitions.definition: To add intoxicating drugs to with the intention of drugging someone.
definitions.example: She suddenly felt strange. She realized her drink must have been drugged.

definitions.definition: To prescribe or administer drugs or medicines.
A drudge.
(gaming slang) micromanagement
(gaming slang) to micromanage
Small, relatively small; used to contrast levels of the noun modified.
definitions.example: At the micro level he was a good manager. At the macro level he failed.

definitions.definition: A computer designed around a microprocessor, smaller than a minicomputer or a mainframe.
The field of economics that deals with small-scale economic activities such as those of an individual or company.
A single unvaried tone of speech or a sound.
definitions.example: When Tima felt like her parents were treating her like a servant, she would speak in monotone and act as though she were a robot.

definitions.definition: A piece of writing in one strain throughout.
To speak in a monotone.
(of speech or a sound) Having a single unvaried pitch.
Being, or having the salient properties of, a monotone function.
definitions.example: The function f(x):=x^3 is monotone on \R, while g(x):=x^2 is not.

definitions.definition: The vocabulary of a language.
A dictionary that includes or focuses on lexemes.
A dictionary of Classical Greek, Hebrew, Latin, or Aramaic.
The lexicology of a programming language. (Usually called lexical structure.)
Any dictionary.
The vocabulary used by or known to an individual. (Also called lexical knowledge)
A vocabulary specific to a certain subject.
definitions.example: a baseball lexicon

definitions.definition: A reason, explanation, or excuse which provides convincing, morally acceptable support for behavior or for a belief or occurrence.
The alignment of text to the left margin (left justification), the right margin (right justification), or both margins (full justification).
A state of terror excited by the sudden appearance of danger; sudden and violent fear, usually of short duration; a sudden alarm.
Anything strange, ugly or shocking, producing a feeling of alarm or aversion.
To frighten.
In a neat manner.
A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.
definitions.example: bodily organs

definitions.definition: (by extension) A body of an organization dedicated to the performing of certain functions.
A musical instrument that has multiple pipes which play when a key is pressed (the pipe organ), or an electronic instrument designed to replicate such.
An official magazine, newsletter, or similar publication of an organization.
Short for organ pipe cactus.
The penis.
To supply with an organ or organs; to fit with organs.
A person of major significance, accomplishment or acclaim.
definitions.example: Newton and Einstein are two of the greats of the history of science.

definitions.definition: The main division in a pipe organ, usually the loudest division.
(in combinations such as "two-greats", "three-greats" etc.) An instance of the word "great" signifying an additional generation in phrases expressing family relationships.
definitions.example: My three-greats grandmother.

definitions.definition: Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!

definitions.definition: Very well (in a very satisfactory manner).
definitions.example: Those mechanical colored pencils work great because they don't have to be sharpened.

definitions.definition: Expression of gladness and content about something.
definitions.example: Great! Thanks for the wonderful work.

definitions.definition: Sarcastic inversion thereof.
definitions.example: Oh, great! I just dumped all 500 sheets of the manuscript all over and now I have to put them back in order.

definitions.definition: The rising or ascent of an aircraft or rocket into flight.
definitions.example: The flight was smooth, but the takeoff was a little rough.

definitions.definition: A parody or lampoon of someone or something.
A quantification, especially of building materials.
definitions.example: I'll give you an estimate after I do the quantity takeoffs for the trusses and structural steel.

definitions.definition: The removal of sheets from the press.
The spot from which one takes off; specifically, the place from which a jumper rises in leaping.
A poison carried by an animal, usually injected into an enemy or prey by biting or stinging.
Feeling or speech marked by spite or malice; vitriol.
To infect with venom; to envenom; to poison.
Poisonous, poisoned; (figuratively) pernicious.
To render valid.
To check or prove the validity of; verify.
To have its validity successfully proven.
definitions.example: The data file cannot be imported because it doesn't validate.

definitions.definition: The unmowed part of a golf course.
A rude fellow; a coarse bully; a rowdy.
A scuffed and roughened area of the pitch, where the bowler's feet fall, used as a target by spin bowlers because of its unpredictable bounce.
The raw material from which faceted or cabochon gems are created.

definitions.definition: The way in which something is positioned vis-à-vis its surroundings.
definitions.example: The Botanical Gardens are in a delightful situation on the river bank.

definitions.definition: The place in which something is situated; a location.
Position or status with regard to conditions and circumstances.
A position of employment; a post.
A difficult or unpleasant set of circumstances; a problem.
definitions.example: Boss, we've got a situation here...

definitions.definition: Free transfer
The usual means of restarting play after a foul is committed, where the non-offending team restarts from where the foul was committed.
To make free; set at liberty; release.
To rid of something that confines or oppresses.
(social) Unconstrained.
definitions.example: He was given free rein to do whatever he wanted.

definitions.definition: Obtainable without any payment.
definitions.example: It's free real estate.

definitions.definition: (abstract) Unconstrained.
(physical) Unconstrained.
Without; not containing (what is specified); exempt; clear; liberated.
definitions.example: We had a wholesome, filling meal, free of meat.  I would like to live free from care in the mountains.

definitions.definition: Ready; eager; acting without spurring or whipping; spirited.
definitions.example: a free horse

definitions.definition: Invested with a particular freedom or franchise; enjoying certain immunities or privileges; admitted to special rights; followed by of.
Certain or honourable; the opposite of base.
definitions.example: free service;  free socage

definitions.definition: Privileged or individual; the opposite of common.
definitions.example: a free fishery;  a free warren

definitions.definition: Without needing to pay.
definitions.example: I got this bike free.

definitions.definition: Freely; willingly.

(other ballgames) a kick in which a player may kick the ball without interference from the opposition. Such a kick may be awarded for a foul by the opposition, or earned by a player such as by taking a mark.
The usual means of restarting play after a foul is committed, where the non-offending team restarts from where the foul was committed.
The selection of desirable items and rejection of undesirable ones in order to obtain an advantage or present something in the best possible light.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
To stand; to tolerate.
A session of being immersed in a tank.

A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.
A weapon designed with the aforementioned specifications intended for slashing and/or stabbing and too short to be called a sword. A dagger.
Any blade-like part in a tool or a machine designed for cutting, such as that of a chipper.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
